user_outcome: Running a bare `sworn loop` (no flags at all) resolves the active release from S01's store and drives it exactly as `sworn loop --release <name>` (S02) does — restoring the coach-loop `coach use <release>` + `coach loop` two-command ergonomics on sworn's own CLI.
rationale: S01 gives sworn a persisted active-release pointer; S02 makes `--release <name>` work standalone. This slice is the small join: when `sworn loop` is invoked with neither `--task` nor `--release`, read the active release from S01's settings table and feed it through the exact same code path S02 established for an explicit `--release`, rather than duplicating dispatch logic. Per the planning decision, this is purely a convenience default — it is not a concurrency mechanism (S02's explicit `--release` remains the way to run multiple releases' loops at once) and does not need to disambiguate between multiple actionable releases.

effort_complexity.rationale: Read one value from the S01 settings table, feed it into the exact dispatch path S02 built for an explicit --release. No new dispatch logic.

acceptance_criteria.text: When `sworn loop` is run with neither `--task` nor `--release`, and an active release is set (via `sworn use`, S01), it SHALL resolve that release from the settings table and drive it through the identical path `sworn loop --release <name>` (S02) uses — no behavioral difference beyond the source of the release name.

If `sworn loop` is run with neither `--task` nor `--release`, and NO active release is set, it SHALL fail closed with a clear, actionable error (e.g. "no active release selected — run `sworn use <release>` first"), not the current generic "--task is required" usage error.

An explicit `--release <name>` flag SHALL always take precedence over the stored active release when both are present — the store is a default for the omitted case only, never an override of an explicit flag.

After the change, `go build ./...` SHALL succeed and `go test ./cmd/sworn/...` SHALL pass, including a reachability test that runs the bare-`sworn loop` dispatch path end-to-end against a fake/stub active-release store (no real model call), proving AC-01 and AC-02 render through the actual CLI entry point, not a leaf helper function in isolation.
